info.name: DabApp — Trainer & Reservation API
info.description:
# DabApp Trainer & Reservation Module

**Version:** 2.0 — June 2026  
**Backend:** Laravel 12 · PHP 8.2 · JWT Auth · PayTabs

---

## How to use this collection

1. Import **DabApp-Environment.postman_environment.json** first
2. Select the **DabApp — Local Dev** environment
3. Run **POST /api/login** → the `TOKEN` variable is auto-set by the test script
4. All authenticated requests auto-inject `Authorization: Bearer {{TOKEN}}`

## Response envelope

All responses follow:
```json
{
  "success": true | false,
  "message": "Human readable text",
  "data": { ... } | [ ... ] | null
}
```

## HTTP Status codes
| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 200 | Success |
| 201 | Created |
| 400 | Business logic error |
| 401 | Not authenticated |
| 403 | Not authorized |
| 404 | Not found |
| 409 | Conflict (duplicate) |
| 422 | Validation error |
| 500 | Server error |

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description:
Register, login, OTP verification, and token management.

**Flow:**
1. `POST /register` → OTP sent to phone
2. `POST /verify-otp` → returns access_token
3. Store token in `{{TOKEN}}` variable
4. Use `POST /refresh` when token expires (after 3600s)

request.description:
Register a new user account. An OTP is sent via WhatsApp/SMS to the phone number.

**Check Laravel logs for OTP in dev:** `storage/logs/laravel.log`

**After registration:** Call `POST /verify-otp` to activate the account.

response.name: 201 Created
422 Validation Error

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent to the user's phone.

**Auto-saves `access_token` to `{{TOKEN}}` variable** via test script.

**Dev tip:** Find the OTP in `storage/logs/laravel.log`

response.name: 200 OK — Token returned

request.description:
Login with email + password. Returns a JWT bearer token.

**Token expiry:** 3600 seconds (1 hour). Use `POST /refresh` before expiry.

**Auto-saves token** to `{{TOKEN}}` via test script.

response.name: 200 OK
401 Wrong credentials

request.description:
Refresh an expiring token without requiring re-login.

**Mobile best practice:** Call this automatically on 401 responses, then retry the original request.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Login as admin. Token saved to `{{ADMIN_TOKEN}}`.

Admin endpoints are under `/api/admin/*` and require this token.

item.name: 🌐 Public — Specialties
item.description:
Dynamic specialty list. Fetch on app start and cache locally.

**Mobile tip:** Cache the specialty list at app launch. Refresh on trainer filter screen open.

query.description: Include approved trainer count per specialty

request.description:
Returns all active specialties ordered by `sort_order`.

Use for:
- Filter chips on the trainer list screen
- Multi-select picker in the 'Become a Trainer' wizard

**Cache strategy:** Cache for 24 hours. Invalidate when user opens filter screen.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:

item.name: 🏍️ Public — Browse Trainers
item.description:
Discover approved trainers. All endpoints are public (no auth required).

**Filtering options:**
- By specialty_id (dynamic system)
- By city
- By GPS location (lat/lng/radius_km)
- By minimum rating
- Free text search

**Sort options:** rating, price, experience, sessions

request.description:
Paginated list of all approved, available trainers.

**Pagination:** Use `data.next_page_url` to know if there are more pages.

response.name: 200 OK — Paginated list

request.description:
Filter trainers by specialty ID (from the dynamic specialties endpoint).

Also supports `specialty=coaching` for legacy slug-based filtering.

Find trainers whose locations are within `radius_km` of the user's GPS position.

**Mobile implementation:**
1. Request location permission
2. Get current position
3. Pass lat/lng to this endpoint
4. Show distance on each card if `distance_km` is returned

Full trainer profile including:
- `locations[]` — training venues with GPS
- `schedules[]` — weekly working hours
- `specialties_list[]` — dynamic specialties
- `gallery[]` — showcase images
- `reviews[]` — last 10 approved reviews
- `is_liked_by_auth` — only when authenticated
- `is_favorited_by_auth` — only when authenticated

response.name: 200 OK — Full profile
404 Not Found
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "Trainer not found" }

request.description:
Returns the trainer's gallery images ordered by sort_order.

**Note:** Gallery is already included in `GET /api/trainers/{id}` response. Use this endpoint only when you need the gallery independently.

response.name: 200 OK
200 OK with rating summary

request.description:
All approved training locations — use as map pins.

**With geo search** (`lat` + `lng`): results include `distance_km` and are sorted by proximity.

item.name: 📅 Availability
item.description:
Check trainer time slot availability before booking.

**Typical flow:**
1. User selects a trainer
2. App fetches availability for the next 7 days
3. Renders a weekly calendar UI
4. User taps a slot → booking form pre-filled

request.description:
Returns available time slots per day for the requested range.

**Key fields:**
- `time_slots[]` — format `"HH:MM-HH:MM"` e.g. `"08:00-10:00"`
- `schedule_source` — `"configured"` = trainer set hours, `"default"` = fallback 08:00–19:00
- Already-booked slots are excluded

**Validation:**
- `from_date` is required
- `to_date` must be ≥ `from_date`
- Dates must be in the future

response.name: 200 OK — Slots available
422 Missing from_date

item.name: 📋 Client — Bookings
item.description:
Book sessions, manage bookings, and initiate payment.

**⚠️ Auto-confirm flow (as of v2.0):**
Bookings are **immediately confirmed** at creation — no trainer manual accept step.
A `payment_url` is returned directly. Redirect the user to it.

**Booking lifecycle:**
```
confirmed → [PayTabs] → paid → in_progress → completed
```

body.raw:
{
  "booking_date": "2026-06-25",
  "start_time": "10:00",
  "duration_hours": 2,
  "location_id": 5,
  "session_type": "beginner",
  "notes": "First time on a track. Please cover safety basics first."
}

request.description:
**Creates a confirmed booking and returns a PayTabs payment URL immediately.**

**Fields:**
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `booking_date` | date | ✅ | YYYY-MM-DD, must be future |
| `start_time` | string | ✅ | HH:MM format (24h) |
| `duration_hours` | integer | No | 1–4, default 1 |
| `location_id` | integer | ✅ | Must belong to this trainer |
| `session_type` | string | No | beginner/intermediate/advanced/custom |
| `notes` | string | No | Max 1000 chars |

**After booking:**
- Open `data.payment_url` in WebView
- Monitor URL for return URL pattern
- `?status=success` → booking paid
- `?status=failed` → retry payment

response.name: 201 Created — Payment URL returned
400 — Slot already booked
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "This time slot is already booked" }

response.name: 400 — Trainer unavailable
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "Trainer is not currently available" }

response.name: 400 — Invalid location
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "Invalid location for this trainer" }

response.name: 422 — Validation error

request.description:
List of the authenticated user's bookings.

**Key field `can_review`:** `true` when session is completed and not yet reviewed → show 'Leave Review' button.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Re-initiate payment for a confirmed booking (if the previous payment attempt failed).

Booking must be `confirmed`. Returns a new PayTabs payment URL.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Cancel a booking. Only allowed when status is `confirmed`.

**If payment was made:** A refund is triggered automatically via PayTabs.

**Cancellation policy:** Defined by platform — currently no penalty.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: { "success": true, "message": "Booking cancelled successfully" }

response.name: 400 — Cannot cancel
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "This booking cannot be cancelled in its current status" }

request.description:
Submit a review for a completed session.

**Constraints:**
- `can_review` must be `true` in booking response
- Session must be `completed`
- Only one review per booking allowed

**rating:** integer 1–5
**comment:** optional, max 2000 chars

response.name: 201 Created
response.body: { "success": true, "message": "Review submitted successfully." }

response.name: 400 — Already reviewed
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "You have already reviewed this session." }

item.name: 👤 Trainer — Become a Trainer
item.description:
Full flow to register as a trainer provider.

**Recommended order:**
1. Upload photo → get `photo_path`
2. Upload cover → get `cover_path`
3. Upload certificates → get `certification_files[]`
4. POST /register with all paths
5. Add locations (one by one)
6. Set schedule
7. Upload gallery

request.description:
Upload the trainer's profile photo. Returns a `path` to use in the registration body.

**Processing:** Auto-resized to max 1200×800px, quality 85%, watermark added.

**Returns:** `path` (relative) + `url` (full public URL) + `thumbnail_url`

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Upload certification files. Returns `paths[]` array to include in registration.

**Accepted formats:** PDF, JPG, PNG
**Max size:** 5MB per file
**Max files:** 10 per request

response.name: 200 OK

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Ahmed Al-Rashidi",
  "name_ar": "أحمد الراشدي",
  "bio": "Professional motorcycle coach with 5 years on-track experience. Specialised in beginner safety and advanced technique.",
  "bio_ar": "مدرب دراجات نارية محترف مع 5 سنوات على الحلبة.",
  "experience_years": 5,
  "price_per_hour": 200.00,
  "certifications": "FIM Level 1, MSF Certified",
  "specialty_ids": [1, 3],
  "photo_path": "trainers/photos/abc123def456.jpg",
  "cover_path": "trainers/covers/xyz789ghi012.jpg",
  "certification_files": [
    "trainers/certificates/cert1.pdf",
    "trainers/certificates/cert2.jpg"
  ]
}

request.description:
**Create a trainer profile.** Profile starts in `pending` status — invisible publicly until admin approves.

**Required fields:** `name`, `experience_years`, `price_per_hour`

**Key field `specialty_ids[]`:** Array of specialty IDs from `GET /api/specialties`. Supports multiple specialties.

**`photo_path` / `cover_path`:** Paths returned by upload endpoints (steps 1 & 2). Alternatively, you can upload the file directly in this request as `photo` / `cover` multipart fields.

**Status after submit:** `pending` → admin must approve before profile appears publicly.

response.name: 201 Created — Pending review
409 Already a trainer
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "You already have a trainer profile" }

request.description: Get the authenticated user's full trainer profile including specialties, gallery, locations, and schedule.

Update any trainer profile fields. Send only the fields to update.

**Toggle availability:** `is_available: 1` (available) or `is_available: 0` (offline)

**Update specialties:** Send `specialty_ids[]` array — replaces current specialties entirely.

**To update photo:** Include `photo_path` (path from upload endpoint) or send file as `photo` (multipart)

**To clear certification files:** Add `"certification_files_empty": true`

item.name: 🗓️ Trainer — Schedule
item.description:
Manage weekly working hours. Available time slots for bookings are derived from the schedule.

**Day mapping:** 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, 3=Wednesday, 4=Thursday, 5=Friday, 6=Saturday

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Upsert the trainer's weekly working hours. This is an **upsert** — send all 7 days.

**Effect on bookings:** Time slots for booking are generated from the configured schedule.

**Set `is_available: false`** to mark a day as closed without removing its record.

item.name: 🗺️ Trainer — Locations
item.description:
Manage training locations. Each booking requires a location_id.

**Multi-location support:** A trainer can have multiple locations. Clients select one when booking.

request.description:
Add a training location. Saves `location_id` to `{{LOCATION_ID}}`.

**Required:** `location_name`, `city_id`
**Optional:** `location_name_ar`, `latitude`, `longitude` (GPS for map display)

response.name: 201 Created

item.name: 🖼️ Trainer — Gallery
item.description:
Manage the trainer's showcase image gallery.

**Flow:**
1. `POST /trainer/gallery` → upload images
2. `POST /trainer/gallery/reorder` → set display order
3. `PATCH /trainer/gallery/{id}` → update captions
4. `DELETE /trainer/gallery/{id}` → remove image

**Max:** 20 images per trainer

request.description:
Upload one or multiple gallery images.

**Processing:** Auto-resized to max 1200×900px + thumbnail (400×400px)

**Returns:** Array of gallery items with `url` and `thumbnail_url`

response.name: 201 Created
400 Gallery full
response.body: { "success": false, "message": "Gallery limit is 20 images. You have 19 and are trying to add 5." }

request.description:
Set the display order by sending an array of gallery IDs in the desired order.

The first ID in the array gets `sort_order=0`, second gets `sort_order=1`, etc.
Permanently deletes the image and its thumbnail from storage.
Mark session as started (`in_progress`). Booking must be `confirmed`.
Mark session as completed. Triggers push notification to client to leave a review.
Toggle like on a trainer. Call again to unlike (toggle behavior).

response.name: 200 OK — Liked
response.body: { "success": true, "message": "Trainer liked.", "liked": true, "likes_count": 36 }

response.name: 200 OK — Unliked
response.body: { "success": true, "message": "Like removed.", "liked": false, "likes_count": 35 }

request.description:
Post a comment on a trainer's profile.

**Note:** Comments require admin approval before appearing publicly.

Set `parent_id` to reply to an existing comment.

item.name: 💳 Payments (Webhook — Server-to-Server)
item.description:
⚠️ These are NOT called from the mobile app. They are called by PayTabs servers.

Do NOT call these endpoints from mobile — they are server-to-server only.

request.description:
**DEV ONLY — Simulate PayTabs callback**

In production this is called automatically by PayTabs after payment.

`cart_id` format: `TRAINER_{booking_id}`

`response_status`:
- `A` = Authorised → booking confirmed + payment_status=paid
- `D` = Declined → booking cancelled + payment_status=failed

item.name: 🔧 Admin — Specialties
item.description:
Manage dynamic specialties. All endpoints require admin authentication.

Use `{{ADMIN_TOKEN}}` (from Admin Login).

request.description:
Upload or replace the icon for a specialty.

**Recommended:** SVG for crisp display at all sizes

**Raster images:** Auto-resized to 128×128px
Approve a pending trainer. Profile becomes publicly visible immediately.
